   126→# =============================================================================
   127→# AST Nodes

   129→
   130→class ASTNode:
   131→    pass
   132→
   133→
   134→class SignalRef(ASTNode):
   135→    """Reference to a signal with optional slice/index.
   136→
   137→    name: signal name
   138→    msb: None for scalar reference, otherwise MSB of slice/index
   139→    lsb: None for scalar reference, LSB of slice (for single bit, msb=lsb)
   140→    """
   141→    def __init__(self, name: str, msb: Optional[int] = None, lsb: Optional[int] = None):
   142→        self.name = name
   143→        self.msb = msb
   144→        self.lsb = lsb
   145→
   146→    def __repr__(self):
   147→        if self.msb is None:
   148→            return f"SignalRef({self.name})"
   149→        elif self.msb == self.lsb:
   150→            return f"SignalRef({self.name}[{self.msb}])"
   151→        else:
   152→            return f"SignalRef({self.name}[{self.msb}:{self.lsb}])"
   153→
   154→
   155→class Literal(ASTNode):
   156→    """Literal value with width.
   157→
   158→    value: integer value
   159→    width: bit width
   160→    """
   161→    def __init__(self, value: int, width: int):
   162→        self.value = value
   163→        self.width = width
   164→
   165→    def __repr__(self):
   166→        return f"Literal(0b{self.value:0{self.width}b}, width={self.width})"
   167→
   168→
   169→class Concat(ASTNode):
   170→    """Concatenation: {e1, e2, ..., ek}"""
   171→    def __init__(self, parts: List[ASTNode]):
   172→        self.parts = parts
   173→
   174→    def __repr__(self):
   175→        return f"Concat([{', '.join(repr(p) for p in self.parts)}])"
   176→
   177→
   178→class OpCall(ASTNode):
   179→    """Operator call: OP(arg1, arg2, ...)"""
   180→    def __init__(self, op: str, args: List[ASTNode]):
   181→        self.op = op
   182→        self.args = args
   183→
   184→    def __repr__(self):
   185→        return f"OpCall({self.op}, [{', '.join(repr(a) for a in self.args)}])"
   186→
   187→
   188→# =============================================================================
   189→# Signal Declaration
   190→# =============================================================================
   191→
   192→class SignalDecl:
   193→    """Signal declaration with width information.
   194→
   195→    name: signal name
   196→    sig_type: 'input', 'output', or 'wire'
   197→    msb: MSB of the signal (None for scalar in old format)
   198→    lsb: LSB of the signal (None for scalar in old format)
   199→    """
   200→    def __init__(self, name: str, sig_type: str, msb: Optional[int] = None, lsb: Optional[int] = None):
   201→        self.name = name
   202→        self.sig_type = sig_type
   203→        self.msb = msb
   204→        self.lsb = lsb
   205→
   206→    @property
   207→    def width(self) -> int:
   208→        if self.msb is None:
   209→            return 1
   210→        return self.msb - self.lsb + 1
   211→
   212→    def __repr__(self):
   213→        if self.msb is None:
   214→            return f"SignalDecl({self.name}, {self.sig_type})"
   215→        return f"SignalDecl({self.name}[{self.msb}:{self.lsb}], {self.sig_type})"

   219→# Token Types
   220→# =============================================================================
   221→
   222→TOKEN_ID = 'ID'
   223→TOKEN_KW = 'KW'
   224→TOKEN_OP = 'OP'
   225→TOKEN_LITERAL = 'LITERAL'
   226→TOKEN_NUMBER = 'NUMBER'
   227→TOKEN_LBRACE = '{'
   228→TOKEN_RBRACE = '}'
   229→TOKEN_LBRACK = '['
   230→TOKEN_RBRACK = ']'
   231→TOKEN_COLON = ':'
   232→TOKEN_EQ = '='
   233→TOKEN_COMMA = ','
   234→TOKEN_LPAREN = '('
   235→TOKEN_RPAREN = ')'
   236→TOKEN_EOF = 'EOF'
   237→
   238→
   239→class Token:
   240→    def __init__(self, type: str, value: str, line: int, col: int):
   241→        self.type = type
   242→        self.value = value
   243→        self.line = line
   244→        self.col = col
   245→
   246→    def __repr__(self):
   247→        return f"Token({self.type}, {self.value!r}, line={self.line}, col={self.col})"
   248→
   249→
   250→# =============================================================================
   251→# Lexer
   252→# =============================================================================
   253→
   254→def tokenize(line: str, line_num: int) -> List[Token]:
   255→    """Tokenize a single line.
   256→
   257→    Returns list of Token objects.
   258→    """
   259→    tokens = []
   260→    pos = 0
   261→    length = len(line)
   262→
   263→    # Operator patterns (case-insensitive)
   264→    operators = {'AND', 'OR', 'XOR', 'NAND', 'NOR', 'XNOR', 'NOT', 'BUF',
   265→                 'MUX', 'ITE', 'REDUCE_AND', 'REDUCE_OR', 'REDUCE_XOR', 'EQ'}
   266→
   267→    while pos < length:
   268→        col_pos = pos + 1  # 1-based column
   269→
   270→        # Skip whitespace
   271→        if line[pos] in ' \t':
   272→            pos += 1
   273→            continue
   274→
   275→        # Comment
   276→        if line[pos] == '#':
   277→            break  # Rest of line is comment, ignore
   278→
   279→        # Identifiers (letters, underscores, digits after first char)
   280→        if re.match(r'[a-zA-Z_]', line[pos:]):
   281→            match = re.match(r'[a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_]*', line[pos:])
   282→            if match:
   283→                token = match.group()
   284→                upper_token = token.upper()
   285→                if upper_token in operators:
   286→                    tokens.append(Token(TOKEN_OP, upper_token, line_num, col_pos))
   287→                elif upper_token in {'INPUT', 'OUTPUT', 'WIRE'}:
   288→                    tokens.append(Token(TOKEN_KW, upper_token, line_num, col_pos))
   289→                else:
   290→                    tokens.append(Token(TOKEN_ID, token, line_num, col_pos))
   291→                pos += len(token)
   292→                continue
   293→
   294→        # Numbers (decimal integers for msb:lsb notation)
   295→        if re.match(r'[0-9]', line[pos:]):
   296→            match = re.match(r'[0-9]+', line[pos:])
   297→            if match:
   298→                tokens.append(Token(TOKEN_NUMBER, match.group(), line_num, col_pos))
   299→                pos += len(match.group())
   300→                continue
   301→
   302→        # Literals: 0, 1 (scalar), sized/unsized vectors
   303→        if line[pos] in '01':
   304→            # Check if this is a sized literal like 8'b1010
   305→            rest = line[pos:]
   306→            sized_match = re.match(r'(\d+)\'[bBhHdD]([\d01a-fA-F_]+)', rest)
   307→            if sized_match:
   308→                # This is a sized literal
   309→                width = int(sized_match.group(1))
   310→                radix_str = sized_match.group(2).lower()
   311→                value_str = sized_match.group(3).replace('_', '')
   312→
   313→                # Parse based on radix
   314→                if radix_str in ('b', 'h', 'd'):
   315→                    try:
   316→                        radix_map = {'b': 2, 'h': 16, 'd': 10}
   317→                        value = int(value_str, radix_map[radix_str])
   318→                    except ValueError:
   319→                        raise CircParseError(
   320→                            f"Invalid literal: {sized_match.group(0)}",
   321→                            file=None, line=line_num, col=col_pos
   322→                        )
   323→
   324→                    # Check value fits in width
   325→                    if value >= (1 << width):
   326→                        raise CircParseError(
   327→                            f"Value {value} too large for {width}-bit literal",
   328→                            file=None, line=line_num, col=col_pos
   329→                        )
   330→
   331→                    tokens.append(Token(TOKEN_LITERAL, f"{width}'{radix_str}{value_str}", line_num, col_pos))
   332→                    pos += len(sized_match.group(0))
   333→                    continue
   334→            else:
   335→                # Scalar literal
   336→                tokens.append(Token(TOKEN_LITERAL, line[pos], line_num, col_pos))
   337→                pos += 1
   338→                continue
   339→
   340→        # Check for 0b or 0x binary/hex literals
   341→        if line[pos:pos+2] in ('0b', '0x'):
   342→            prefix = line[pos:pos+2]
   343→            rest = line[pos+2:]
   344→            # Must have at least one digit after prefix
   345→            match = re.match(r'[0-9a-fA-F]+', rest)
   346→            if match:
   347→                digits = match.group()
   348→                tokens.append(Token(TOKEN_LITERAL, f"{prefix}{digits}", line_num, col_pos))
   349→                pos += 2 + len(digits)
   350→                continue
   351→            else:
   352→                raise CircParseError(
   353→                    f"Binary/hex literal must have at least one digit",
   354→                    file=None, line=line_num, col=col_pos
   355→                )
   356→
   357→        # Braces and brackets
   358→        if line[pos] in '{}[]':
   359→            tokens.append(Token(line[pos], line[pos], line_num, col_pos))
   360→            pos += 1
   361→            continue
   362→
   363→        # Colon
   364→        if line[pos] == ':':
   365→            tokens.append(Token(TOKEN_COLON, ':', line_num, col_pos))
   366→            pos += 1
   367→            continue
   368→
   369→        # Comma
   370→        if line[pos] == ',':
   371→            tokens.append(Token(TOKEN_COMMA, ',', line_num, col_pos))
   372→            pos += 1
   373→            continue
   374→
   375→        # Equals sign
   376→        if line[pos] == '=':
   377→            tokens.append(Token(TOKEN_EQ, '=', line_num, col_pos))
   378→            pos += 1
   379→            continue
   380→
   381→        # Parentheses
   382→        if line[pos] in '()':
   383→            type_map = {'(': TOKEN_LPAREN, ')': TOKEN_RPAREN}
   384→            tokens.append(Token(type_map[line[pos]], line[pos], line_num, col_pos))
   385→            pos += 1
   386→            continue
   387→
   388→        # Unknown character
   389→        raise CircParseError(
   390→            f"Unexpected character: {line[pos]}",
   391→            file=None, line=line_num, col=col_pos
   392→        )
   393→
   394→    tokens.append(Token(TOKEN_EOF, '', line_num, len(line) + 1))
   395→    return tokens
   396→
   397→
   398→# =============================================================================
   399→# Parser
   400→# =============================================================================
   401→
   402→class Parser:
   403→    """Parser for circuit expressions."""
   404→
   405→    def __init__(self, tokens: List[Token], filepath: str):
   406→        self.tokens = tokens
   407→        self.filepath = filepath
   408→        self.pos = 0
   409→
   410→    def peek(self) -> Token:
   411→        return self.tokens[self.pos]
   412→
   413→    def consume(self, expected_type: Optional[str] = None,
   414→                expected_value: Optional[str] = None) -> Token:
   415→        tok = self.peek()
   416→        if expected_type is not None and tok.type != expected_type:
   417→            raise self.error(f"Expected token type {expected_type}, got {tok.type}")
   418→        if expected_value is not None and tok.value != expected_value:
   419→            raise self.error(f"Expected {expected_value}, got {tok.value}")
   420→        self.pos += 1
   421→        return tok
   422→
   423→    def error(self, message: str) -> CircParseError:
   424→        tok = self.peek()
   425→        return CircParseError(
   426→            message,
   427→            file=self.filepath,
   428→            line=tok.line,
   429→            col=tok.col
   430→        )
   431→
   432→    def parse_expression(self) -> ASTNode:
   433→        """Parse an expression: concatenation, signal ref, literal, or op call."""
   434→        tok = self.peek()
   435→
   436→        # If it's an identifier followed by [, it's a slice/index
   437→        if tok.type == TOKEN_ID:
   438→            name = tok.value
   439→            self.consume(TOKEN_ID)
   440→
   441→            # Check for slice or index
   442→            if self.peek().type == TOKEN_LBRACK:
   443→                return self.parse_signal_ref_with_select(name)
   444→            else:
   445→                return SignalRef(name)
   446→
   447→        elif tok.type == TOKEN_LITERAL:
   448→            return self.parse_literal()
   449→
   450→        elif tok.type == TOKEN_LBRACE:
   451→            # Concatenation: {e1, e2, ...}
   452→            return self.parse_concat()
   453→
   454→        elif tok.type == TOKEN_OP:
   455→            # Operator call: OP(...)
   456→            return self.parse_op_call()
   457→
   458→        else:
   459→            raise self.error(f"Unexpected token in expression: {tok.value}")
   460→
   461→    def parse_signal_ref_with_select(self, name: str) -> SignalRef:
   462→        """Parse a signal reference with optional slice/index: v[i] or v[hi:lo]"""
   463→        self.consume(TOKEN_LBRACK)
   464→
   465→        # Parse first number (msb or index)
   466→        if self.peek().type != TOKEN_NUMBER:
   467→            raise self.error("Expected number in slice/index")
   468→        msb_val = int(self.consume().value)
   469→
   470→        # Check if it's a slice (with colon) or single bit index
   471→        if self.peek().type == TOKEN_COLON:
   472→            self.consume(TOKEN_COLON)  # consume ':'
   473→
   474→            # Parse lsb
   475→            if self.peek().type != TOKEN_NUMBER:
   476→                raise self.error("Expected number after colon in slice")
   477→            lsb_val = int(self.consume().value)
   478→
   479→            self.consume(TOKEN_RBRACK)
   480→
   481→            # Validate: msb >= lsb >= 0
   482→            if msb_val < lsb_val:
   483→                raise self.error(f"Invalid slice: msb ({msb_val}) < lsb ({lsb_val})")
   484→            if lsb_val < 0:
   485→                raise self.error(f"Invalid slice: lsb ({lsb_val}) < 0")
   486→
   487→            return SignalRef(name, msb_val, lsb_val)
   488→        else:
   489→            # Single bit index
   490→            self.consume(TOKEN_RBRACK)
   491→            return SignalRef(name, msb_val, msb_val)  # msb=lsb for single bit
   492→
   493→    def parse_literal(self) -> Literal:
   494→        """Parse a literal value."""
   495→        tok = self.consume(TOKEN_LITERAL)
   496→        val = tok.value
   497→
   498→        if val in ('0', '1'):
   499→            return Literal(int(val), 1)
   500→        elif val.startswith('0b'):
   501→            # Binary literal: 0b1101
   502→            bits = val[2:]
   503→            if not bits:
   504→                raise self.error("Binary literal must have at least one digit")
   505→            value = int(bits, 2)
   506→            return Literal(value, len(bits))
   507→        elif val.startswith('0x'):
   508→            # Hex literal: 0x0f
   509→            hex_digits = val[2:]
   510→            if not hex_digits:
   511→                raise self.error("Hex literal must have at least one digit")
   512→            value = int(hex_digits, 16)
   513→            return Literal(value, len(hex_digits) * 4)
   514→        else:
   515→            # Sized literal: 8'b1010, 8'hff, 8'd200
   516→            match = re.match(r'(\d+)\'[bBhHdD]([\d01a-fA-F_]+)', val)
   517→            if match:
   518→                width = int(match.group(1))
   519→                radix_str = match.group(2).lower()
   520→                value_str = match.group(3).replace('_', '')
   521→
   522→                try:
   523→                    radix_map = {'b': 2, 'h': 16, 'd': 10}
   524→                    value = int(value_str, radix_map[radix_str])
   525→                except ValueError:
   526→                    raise self.error(f"Invalid literal: {val}")
   527→
   528→                if value >= (1 << width):
   529→                    raise self.error(f"Value {value} too large for {width}-bit literal")
   530→
   531→                return Literal(value, width)
   532→            else:
   533→                raise self.error(f"Invalid literal format: {val}")
   534→
   535→    def parse_concat(self) -> Concat:
   536→        """Parse concatenation: {e1, e2, ...}"""
   537→        self.consume(TOKEN_LBRACE)
   538→
   539→        parts = []
   540→        while True:
   541→            if self.peek().type == TOKEN_RBRACE:
   542→                break
   543→
   544→            parts.append(self.parse_expression())
   545→
   546→            if self.peek().type == TOKEN_COMMA:
   547→                self.consume(TOKEN_COMMA)
   548→                continue
   549→            elif self.peek().type == TOKEN_RBRACE:
   550→                break
   551→
   552→        self.consume(TOKEN_RBRACE)
   553→        return Concat(parts)
   554→
   555→    def parse_op_call(self) -> OpCall:
   556→        """Parse operator call: OP(arg1, arg2, ...)"""
   557→        op_tok = self.consume(TOKEN_OP)
   558→        op = op_tok.value
   559→
   560→        self.consume(TOKEN_LPAREN)
   561→
   562→        args = []
   563→        while True:
   564→            if self.peek().type == TOKEN_RPAREN:
   565→                break
   566→
   567→            args.append(self.parse_expression())
   568→
   569→            if self.peek().type == TOKEN_COMMA:
   570→                self.consume(TOKEN_COMMA)
   571→                continue
   572→            elif self.peek().type == TOKEN_RPAREN:
   573→                break
   574→
   575→        self.consume(TOKEN_RPAREN)
   576→        return OpCall(op, args)
   577→
   578→    def parse_assignment_line(self) -> Tuple[str, ASTNode]:
   579→        """Parse an assignment line: lhs = expression"""
   580→        # Parse LHS (identifier)
   581→        lhs_tok = self.consume(TOKEN_ID)
   582→        lhs = lhs_tok.value
   583→
   584→        self.consume(TOKEN_EQ)
   585→
   586→        # Parse RHS expression
   587→        expr = self.parse_expression()
   588→
   589→        # Make sure we're at EOF
   590→        if self.peek().type != TOKEN_EOF:
   591→            raise self.error(f"Unexpected token after expression: {self.peek().value}")
   592→
   593→        return lhs, expr
   594→
   595→    def parse_declaration(self) -> List[SignalDecl]:
   596→        """Parse a declaration line: INPUT/OUTPUT/WIRE name[msb:lsb]"""
   597→        kw_tok = self.consume(TOKEN_KW)
   598→        decl_type = kw_tok.value.lower()
   599→
   600→        decls = []
   601→        while True:
   602→            # Parse identifier
   603→            id_tok = self.consume(TOKEN_ID)
   604→            name = id_tok.value
   605→
   606→            # Check for width specifier
   607→            msb = None
   608→            lsb = None
   609→            if self.peek().type == TOKEN_LBRACK:
   610→                self.consume(TOKEN_LBRACK)
   611→
   612→                # Parse msb
   613→                if self.peek().type != TOKEN_NUMBER:
   614→                    raise self.error(f"Expected number after [ for {name}")
   615→                msb = int(self.consume().value)
   616→
   617→                # Check for slice or single bit
   618→                if self.peek().type == TOKEN_COLON:
   619→                    self.consume(TOKEN_COLON)
   620→
   621→                    if self.peek().type != TOKEN_NUMBER:
   622→                        raise self.error(f"Expected number after : for {name}")
   623→                    lsb = int(self.consume().value)
   624→
   625→                    if msb < lsb:
   626→                        raise self.error(f"Invalid range for {name}: msb ({msb}) < lsb ({lsb})")
   627→                    if lsb < 0:
   628→                        raise self.error(f"Invalid range for {name}: lsb ({lsb}) < 0")
   629→                else:
   630→                    # Single bit - msb=lsb
   631→                    lsb = msb
   632→
   633→                self.consume(TOKEN_RBRACK)
   634→
   635→            decls.append(SignalDecl(name, decl_type, msb, lsb))
   636→
   637→            # Check for more names or end of line
   638→            if self.peek().type == TOKEN_EOF:
   639→                break
   640→            elif self.peek().type == TOKEN_COMMA:
   641→                self.consume(TOKEN_COMMA)
   642→                continue
   643→            elif self.peek().type == TOKEN_ID:
   644→                # Another identifier directly - continue to consume it
   645→                continue
   646→            else:
   647→                raise self.error(f"Unexpected token: {self.peek().value}")
   648→
   649→        return decls
   650→
   651→
   652→def parse_circ_file(filepath: str) -> Tuple[List[SignalDecl], List[Tuple[str, ASTNode]]]:
   653→    """Parse a .circ file and return (declarations, assignments).
   654→
   655→    Returns:
   656→        declarations: List of SignalDecl objects
   657→        assignments: List of (lhs, expression) tuples
   658→    """
   659→    with open(filepath, 'r') as f:
   660→        lines = f.readlines()
   661→
   662→    declarations = []
   663→    assignments = []
   664→    seen_assignment = False
   665→
   666→    for line_num, line in enumerate(lines, 1):
   667→        line = line.rstrip('\n\r')
   668→
   669→        # Skip empty lines and comments
   670→        stripped = line.lstrip()
   671→        if not stripped or stripped.startswith('#'):
   672→            continue
   673→
   674→        try:
   675→            tokens = tokenize(line, line_num)
   676→        except CircParseError as e:
   677→            if e.file is None:
   678→                e.file = filepath
   679→            raise
   680→
   681→        if not tokens:
   682→            continue
   683→
   684→        parser = Parser(tokens, filepath)
   685→
   686→        # Check if it's a declaration
   687→        if tokens[0].type == TOKEN_KW:
   688→            # Declaration line
   689→            if seen_assignment:
   690→                raise DeclarationAfterAssignmentError(
   691→                    f"Declaration found after assignment",
   692→                    file=filepath,
   693→                    line=line_num,
   694→                    col=1
   695→                )
   696→
   697→            try:
   698→                decls = parser.parse_declaration()
   699→                declarations.extend(decls)
   700→            except CircParseError as e:
   701→                if e.file is None:
   702→                    e.file = filepath
   703→                raise
   704→
   705→        elif tokens[0].type == TOKEN_ID:
   706→            # Assignment line
   707→            seen_assignment = True
   708→
   709→            try:
   710→                lhs, expr = parser.parse_assignment_line()
   711→                assignments.append((lhs, expr))
   712→            except CircParseError as e:
   713→                if e.file is None:
   714→                    e.file = filepath
   715→                raise
   716→        else:
   717→            raise CircParseError(
   718→                f"Expected keyword (input/output/wire) or identifier",
   719→                file=filepath,
   720→                line=line_num,
   721→                col=1
   722→            )
   723→
   724→    return declarations, assignments
   725→

   727→# =============================================================================
   728→# JSON Circuit Format Parser
   729→# =============================================================================
   730→
   731→def parse_json_file(filepath: str) -> Tuple[List[SignalDecl], List[Tuple[str, ASTNode]]]:
   732→    """Parse a JSON circuit file and return (declarations, assignments).
   733→
   734→    Returns:
   735→        declarations: List of SignalDecl objects
   736→        assignments: List of (lhs, expression) tuples
   737→
   738→    Raises:
   739→        JsonParseError: Invalid JSON syntax
   740→        JsonSchemaError: JSON doesn't match expected schema
   741→        CircParseError: Expression parsing error
   742→    """
   743→    with open(filepath, 'r') as f:
   744→        try:
   745→            data = json.load(f)
   746→        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
   747→            raise JsonParseError(
   748→                f"Invalid JSON syntax: {e}",
   749→                file=filepath,
   750→                line=e.lineno,
   751→                col=e.colno
   752→            )
   753→
   754→    # Validate schema
   755→    if not isinstance(data, dict):
   756→        raise JsonSchemaError(
   757→            "JSON root must be an object",
   758→            file=filepath
   759→        )
   760→
   761→    required_keys = {'format_version', 'inputs', 'outputs', 'wires', 'assignments'}
   762→    missing = required_keys - set(data.keys())
   763→    if missing:
   764→        raise JsonSchemaError(
   765→            f"Missing required keys: {', '.join(sorted(missing))}",
   766→            file=filepath
   767→        )
   768→
   769→    # Check format_version
   770→    if data['format_version'] != 1:
   771→        raise JsonSchemaError(
   772→            f"Unsupported format_version: {data['format_version']}. Expected 1.",
   773→            file=filepath
   774→        )
   775→
   776→    # Helper to validate port object
   777→    def validate_port(port: dict, key_name: str) -> dict:
   778→        if not isinstance(port, dict):
   779→            raise JsonSchemaError(
   780→                f"{key_name} must be an array of objects",
   781→                file=filepath
   782→            )
   783→        if 'name' not in port:
   784→            raise JsonSchemaError(
   785→                f"Port object missing 'name' field",
   786→                file=filepath
   787→            )
   788→        msb = port.get('msb')
   789→        lsb = port.get('lsb')
   790→
   791→        if msb is None and lsb is None:
   792→            # Scalar port - valid
   793→            return port
   794→
   795→        if not isinstance(msb, int) or not isinstance(lsb, int):
   796→            raise JsonSchemaError(
   797→                f"Port '{port.get('name', '?')}': msb and lsb must be integers",
   798→                file=filepath
   799→            )
   800→
   801→        if msb < lsb:
   802→            raise JsonSchemaError(
   803→                f"Port '{port['name']}': msb ({msb}) < lsb ({lsb})",
   804→                file=filepath
   805→            )
   806→
   807→        if lsb < 0:
   808→            raise JsonSchemaError(
   809→                f"Port '{port['name']}': lsb ({lsb}) < 0",
   810→                file=filepath
   811→            )
   812→
   813→        return port
   814→
   815→    # Parse inputs
   816→    inputs_list = data['inputs']
   817→    if not isinstance(inputs_list, list):
   818→        raise JsonSchemaError(
   819→            "'inputs' must be an array",
   820→            file=filepath
   821→        )
   822→
   823→    declarations: List[SignalDecl] = []
   824→    seen_names: Set[str] = set()
   825→
   826→    for i, port in enumerate(inputs_list):
   827→        validate_port(port, f"inputs[{i}]")
   828→        name = port['name']
   829→        if name in seen_names:
   830→            raise DuplicateNameError(
   831→                f"Duplicate input name: {name}",
   832→                file=filepath
   833→            )
   834→        seen_names.add(name)
   835→
   836→        msb = port.get('msb')
   837→        lsb = port.get('lsb')
   838→        # Convert None/None to None, None for scalar; otherwise keep values
   839→        msb_val = msb if msb is not None else None
   840→        lsb_val = lsb if lsb is not None else None
   841→        declarations.append(SignalDecl(name, 'input', msb_val, lsb_val))
   842→
   843→    # Parse outputs
   844→    outputs_list = data['outputs']
   845→    if not isinstance(outputs_list, list):
   846→        raise JsonSchemaError(
   847→            "'outputs' must be an array",
   848→            file=filepath
   849→        )
   850→
   851→    for i, port in enumerate(outputs_list):
   852→        validate_port(port, f"outputs[{i}]")
   853→        name = port['name']
   854→        if name in seen_names:
   855→            raise DuplicateNameError(
   856→                f"Duplicate output name: {name}",
   857→                file=filepath
   858→            )
   859→        seen_names.add(name)
   860→
   861→        msb = port.get('msb')
   862→        lsb = port.get('lsb')
   863→        msb_val = msb if msb is not None else None
   864→        lsb_val = lsb if lsb is not None else None
   865→        declarations.append(SignalDecl(name, 'output', msb_val, lsb_val))
   866→
   867→    # Parse wires
   868→    wires_list = data['wires']
   869→    if not isinstance(wires_list, list):
   870→        raise JsonSchemaError(
   871→            "'wires' must be an array",
   872→            file=filepath
   873→        )
   874→
   875→    for i, port in enumerate(wires_list):
   876→        validate_port(port, f"wires[{i}]")
   877→        name = port['name']
   878→        if name in seen_names:
   879→            raise DuplicateNameError(
   880→                f"Duplicate wire name: {name}",
   881→                file=filepath
   882→            )
   883→        seen_names.add(name)
   884→
   885→        msb = port.get('msb')
   886→        lsb = port.get('lsb')
   887→        msb_val = msb if msb is not None else None
   888→        lsb_val = lsb if lsb is not None else None
   889→        declarations.append(SignalDecl(name, 'wire', msb_val, lsb_val))
   890→
   891→    # Parse assignments
   892→    assignments_list = data['assignments']
   893→    if not isinstance(assignments_list, list):
   894→        raise JsonSchemaError(
   895→            "'assignments' must be an array",
   896→            file=filepath
   897→        )
   898→
   899→    assignments: List[Tuple[str, ASTNode]] = []
   900→    lhs_seen: Set[str] = set()
   901→
   902→    for i, assn in enumerate(assignments_list):
   903→        if not isinstance(assn, dict):
   904→            raise JsonSchemaError(
   905→                f"assignments[{i}] must be an object",
   906→                file=filepath
   907→            )
   908→        if 'lhs' not in assn:
   909→            raise JsonSchemaError(
   910→                f"assignments[{i}] missing 'lhs' field",
   911→                file=filepath
   912→            )
   913→        if 'rhs' not in assn:
   914→            raise JsonSchemaError(
   915→                f"assignments[{i}] missing 'rhs' field",
   916→                file=filepath
   917→            )
   918→
   919→        lhs = assn['lhs']
   920→        rhs_str = assn['rhs']
   921→
   922→        if not isinstance(lhs, str):
   923→            raise JsonSchemaError(
   924→                f"assignments[{i}].lhs must be a string",
   925→                file=filepath
   926→            )
   927→        if not isinstance(rhs_str, str):
   928→            raise JsonSchemaError(
   929→                f"assignments[{i}].rhs must be a string",
   930→                file=filepath
   931→            )
   932→
   933→        if lhs in lhs_seen:
   934→            raise RedefinitionError(
   935→                f"Signal '{lhs}' defined multiple times",
   936→                file=filepath
   937→            )
   938→        lhs_seen.add(lhs)
   939→
   940→        # Parse RHS expression using existing expression parser
   941→        # Create a temporary tokens list by tokenizing the expression string
   942→        # We'll reuse the existing tokenize function by creating a mock line
   943→        try:
   944→            tokens = tokenize(rhs_str, 1)  # line 1 for expression error context
   945→        except CircParseError as e:
   946→            # Adjust line/col to point to the assignment
   947→            raise CircParseError(
   948→                f"Error in assignment {i}: {e.message}",
   949→                file=filepath,
   950→                line=None,
   951→                col=None
   952→            )
   953→
   954→        parser = Parser(tokens, filepath)
   955→        try:
   956→            expr = parser.parse_expression()
   957→        except CircParseError as e:
   958→            raise CircParseError(
   959→                f"Error in assignment {i}: {e.message}",
   960→                file=filepath
   961→            )
   962→
   963→        # Ensure we consumed all tokens (no trailing junk)
   964→        if parser.peek().type != TOKEN_EOF:
   965→            raise CircParseError(
   966→                f"Unexpected token after expression in assignment {i}",
   967→                file=filepath
   968→            )
   969→
   970→        assignments.append((lhs, expr))
   971→
   972→    # Validate that every wire and output appears exactly once as assignment LHS
   973→    wire_names = {d.name for d in declarations if d.sig_type == 'wire'}
   974→    output_names = {d.name for d in declarations if d.sig_type == 'output'}
   975→    assigned_names = set(lhs_seen)
   976→
   977→    unassigned = (wire_names | output_names) - assigned_names
   978→    if unassigned:
   979→        raise UnassignedSignalError(
   980→            f"Unassigned signals: {', '.join(sorted(unassigned))}",
   981→            file=filepath
   982→        )
   983→
   984→    # Check for extra assignments (LHS not declared)
   985→    declared_names = {d.name for d in declarations}
   986→    extra_assigned = assigned_names - declared_names
   987→    if extra_assigned:
   988→        raise UndefinedNameError(
   989→            f"Assignment to undeclared signal(s): {', '.join(sorted(extra_assigned))}",
   990→            file=filepath
   991→        )
   992→
   993→    return declarations, assignments
   994→
   995→
   996→# =============================================================================
   997→# BENCH Circuit Format Parser
   998→# =============================================================================
   999→
  1000→def parse_bench_file(filepath: str) -> Tuple[List[SignalDecl], List[Tuple[str, ASTNode]]]:
  1001→    """Parse a BENCH format circuit file and return (declarations, assignments).
  1002→
  1003→    BENCH is a scalar-only format commonly used in circuit benchmarks.
  1004→
  1005→    Returns:
  1006→        declarations: List of SignalDecl objects (all scalar)
  1007→        assignments: List of (lhs, expression) tuples
  1008→
  1009→    Raises:
  1010→        BenchParseError: Syntax error in .bench file
  1011→        RedefinitionError: Signal defined multiple times
  1012→        UndefinedNameError: Undefined name referenced
  1013→    """
  1014→    with open(filepath, 'r') as f:
  1015→        lines = f.readlines()
  1016→
  1017→    declarations: List[SignalDecl] = []
  1018→    assignments: List[Tuple[str, ASTNode]] = []
  1019→
  1020→    # Track all signals (inputs, wires, outputs)
  1021→    signal_names: Set[str] = set()
  1022→    input_names: Set[str] = set()
  1023→    output_names: Set[str] = set()
  1024→    wire_names: Set[str] = set()
  1025→
  1026→    # Track assigned signals (for RedefinitionError)
  1027→    assigned_lhs: Set[str] = set()
  1028→
  1029→    # For expression parsing
  1030→    op_arity = {
  1031→        'NOT': 1,
  1032→        'BUF': 1,
  1033→        'AND': 2,
  1034→        'OR': 2,
  1035→        'XOR': 2,
  1036→        'NAND': 2,
  1037→        'NOR': 2,
  1038→        'XNOR': 2,
  1039→    }
  1040→
  1041→    for line_num, line in enumerate(lines, 1):
  1042→        line = line.rstrip('\n\r')
  1043→
  1044→        # Strip comment
  1045→        if '#' in line:
  1046→            line = line[:line.index('#')]
  1047→
  1048→        # Skip empty lines
  1049→        stripped = line.strip()
  1050→        if not stripped:
  1051→            continue
  1052→
  1053→        # Remove all whitespace for easier parsing
  1054→        no_whitespace = ''.join(stripped.split())
  1055→
  1056→        # Check for INPUT(name)
  1057→        if no_whitespace.startswith('INPUT(') and no_whitespace.endswith(')'):
  1058→            name = no_whitespace[6:-1]  # Remove 'INPUT(' and ')'
  1059→            if not name:
  1060→                raise BenchParseError(
  1061→                    f"Empty input name at line {line_num}",
  1062→                    file=filepath,
  1063→                    line=line_num,
  1064→                    col=1
  1065→                )
  1066→
  1067→            # Check for brackets (vector support not allowed in BENCH)
  1068→            if '[' in name or ']' in name:
  1069→                raise BenchParseError(
  1070→                    f"Vector notation not allowed in BENCH: {name}",
  1071→                    file=filepath,
  1072→                    line=line_num,
  1073→                    col=1
  1074→                )
  1075→
  1076→            if name in signal_names:
  1077→                raise RedefinitionError(
  1078→                    f"Duplicate signal definition: {name}",
  1079→                    file=filepath,
  1080→                    line=line_num,
  1081→                    col=1
  1082→                )
  1083→
  1084→            signal_names.add(name)
  1085→            input_names.add(name)
  1086→            declarations.append(SignalDecl(name, 'input', None, None))
  1087→            continue
  1088→
  1089→        # Check for OUTPUT(name)
  1090→        if no_whitespace.startswith('OUTPUT(') and no_whitespace.endswith(')'):
  1091→            name = no_whitespace[7:-1]  # Remove 'OUTPUT(' and ')'
  1092→            if not name:
  1093→                raise BenchParseError(
  1094→                    f"Empty output name at line {line_num}",
  1095→                    file=filepath,
  1096→                    line=line_num,
  1097→                    col=1
  1098→                )
  1099→
  1100→            if '[' in name or ']' in name:
  1101→                raise BenchParseError(
  1102→                    f"Vector notation not allowed in BENCH: {name}",
  1103→                    file=filepath,
  1104→                    line=line_num,
  1105→                    col=1
  1106→                )
  1107→
  1108→            if name in signal_names:
  1109→                raise RedefinitionError(
  1110→                    f"Duplicate signal definition: {name}",
  1111→                    file=filepath,
  1112→                    line=line_num,
  1113→                    col=1
  1114→                )
  1115→
  1116→            signal_names.add(name)
  1117→            output_names.add(name)
  1118→            # Add as output declaration
  1119→            declarations.append(SignalDecl(name, 'output', None, None))
  1120→            continue
  1121→
  1122→        # Check for assignment: lhs = OP(arg1, arg2, ...)
  1123→        if '=' in no_whitespace:
  1124→            parts = no_whitespace.split('=', 1)
  1125→            if len(parts) != 2:
  1126→                raise BenchParseError(
  1127→                    f"Invalid assignment at line {line_num}",
  1128→                    file=filepath,
  1129→                    line=line_num,
  1130→                    col=1
  1131→                )
  1132→
  1133→            lhs = parts[0]
  1134→            rhs = parts[1]
  1135→
  1136→            # Validate LHS
  1137→            if not lhs:
  1138→                raise BenchParseError(
  1139→                    f"Empty LHS in assignment at line {line_num}",
  1140→                    file=filepath,
  1141→                    line=line_num,
  1142→                    col=1
  1143→                )
  1144→
  1145→            if '[' in lhs or ']' in lhs:
  1146→                raise BenchParseError(
  1147→                    f"Vector notation not allowed in BENCH: {lhs}",
  1148→                    file=filepath,
  1149→                    line=line_num,
  1150→                    col=1
  1151→                )
  1152→
  1153→            # Check for redefinition
  1154→            if lhs in assigned_lhs:
  1155→                raise RedefinitionError(
  1156→                    f"Redefinition of signal: {lhs}",
  1157→                    file=filepath,
  1158→                    line=line_num,
  1159→                    col=1
  1160→                )
  1161→
  1162→            assigned_lhs.add(lhs)
  1163→
  1164→            # Add as wire if not already declared as input/output
  1165→            if lhs not in signal_names:
  1166→                signal_names.add(lhs)
  1167→                wire_names.add(lhs)
  1168→                declarations.append(SignalDecl(lhs, 'wire', None, None))
  1169→
  1170→            # Parse RHS: OP(arg1, arg2, ...)
  1171→            # Find operator name
  1172→            op_match = None
  1173→            for op in op_arity:
  1174→                if rhs.startswith(op + '(') and rhs.endswith(')'):
  1175→                    op_match = op
  1176→                    break
  1177→
  1178→            if op_match is None:
  1179→                raise BenchParseError(
  1180→                    f"Unknown or malformed operator call: {rhs}",
  1181→                    file=filepath,
  1182→                    line=line_num,
  1183→                    col=1
  1184→                )
  1185→
  1186→            args_str = rhs[len(op_match)+1:-1]  # Remove OP( and )
  1187→            if args_str:
  1188→                arg_names = [arg.strip() for arg in args_str.split(',')]
  1189→            else:
  1190→                arg_names = []
  1191→
  1192→            # Validate argument count
  1193→            min_arity = op_arity[op_match]
  1194→            if len(arg_names) < min_arity:
  1195→                raise BenchParseError(
  1196→                    f"Operator {op_match} requires at least {min_arity} operands, got {len(arg_names)}",
  1197→                    file=filepath,
  1198→                    line=line_num,
  1199→                    col=1
  1200→                )
  1201→
  1202→            # Check for literals (BENCH doesn't allow literals in RHS)
  1203→            for arg in arg_names:
  1204→                # Literals: 0, 1, 0b..., 0x..., sized literals
  1205→                if arg in ('0', '1'):
  1206→                    raise BenchParseError(
  1207→                        f"Literals not allowed in BENCH: {arg}",
  1208→                        file=filepath,
  1209→                        line=line_num,
  1210→                        col=1
  1211→                    )
  1212→                if arg.startswith('0b') or arg.startswith('0x'):
  1213→                    raise BenchParseError(
  1214→                        f"Literals not allowed in BENCH: {arg}",
  1215→                        file=filepath,
  1216→                        line=line_num,
  1217→                        col=1
  1218→                    )
  1219→                if re.match(r'^\d+[\'[bBhHdD]', arg):
  1220→                    raise BenchParseError(
  1221→                        f"Literals not allowed in BENCH: {arg}",
  1222→                        file=filepath,
  1223→                        line=line_num,
  1224→                        col=1
  1225→                    )
  1226→
  1227→                # Check for brackets (vector not allowed)
  1228→                if '[' in arg or ']' in arg:
  1229→                    raise BenchParseError(
  1230→                        f"Vector notation not allowed in BENCH: {arg}",
  1231→                        file=filepath,
  1232→                        line=line_num,
  1233→                        col=1
  1234→                    )
  1235→
  1236→            # Build AST: OpCall with SignalRef arguments
  1237→            args_ast = []
  1238→            for arg in arg_names:
  1239→                # BENCH is scalar-only, no indices/slices
  1240→                args_ast.append(SignalRef(arg))
  1241→
  1242→            expr = OpCall(op_match, args_ast)
  1243→            assignments.append((lhs, expr))
  1244→            continue
  1245→
  1246→        # If we get here, the line is invalid
  1247→        raise BenchParseError(
  1248→            f"Invalid statement: {stripped}",
  1249→            file=filepath,
  1250→            line=line_num,
  1251→            col=1
  1252→        )
  1253→
  1254→    # Validate OUTPUT references
  1255→    for out_name in output_names:
  1256→        if out_name not in signal_names:
  1257→            raise UndefinedNameError(
  1258→                f"OUTPUT references undefined signal: {out_name}",
  1259→                file=filepath
  1260→            )
  1261→        # Also check that OUTPUT is assigned somewhere (either as input or assigned)
  1262→        if out_name not in assigned_lhs and out_name not in input_names:
  1263→            raise UndefinedNameError(
  1264→                f"OUTPUT references undefined signal: {out_name}",
  1265→                file=filepath
  1266→            )
  1267→
  1268→    # Validate that all assigned signals (except inputs) are defined
  1269→    for lhs in assigned_lhs:
  1270→        if lhs not in input_names and lhs not in wire_names:
  1271→            # Already added as wire, but check consistency
  1272→            pass
  1273→
  1274→    # Also: wires must be assigned (already tracked via assigned_lhs)
  1275→    for wname in wire_names:
  1276→        if wname not in assigned_lhs:
  1277→            raise UnassignedSignalError(
  1278→                f"Unassigned wire: {wname}",
  1279→                file=filepath
  1280→            )
  1281→
  1282→    return declarations, assignments
  1283→
  1284→
  1285→# =============================================================================
  1286→# Expression Evaluator (for circuit evaluation)
  1287→# =============================================================================
  1288→
  1289→def eval_expression(expr: ASTNode, signal_values: Dict[str, int],
  1290→                    signal_widths: Dict[str, int],
  1291→                    filepath: str) -> Tuple[int, int]:
  1292→    """Evaluate an expression and return (value, width).
  1293→
  1294→    Args:
  1295→        expr: AST node to evaluate
  1296→        signal_values: Dict mapping signal names to their integer values
  1297→        signal_widths: Dict mapping signal names to their widths
  1298→        filepath: For error reporting
  1299→
  1300→    Returns:
  1301→        Tuple of (value, width)
  1302→    """
  1303→    if isinstance(expr, SignalRef):
  1304→        if expr.name in signal_values:
  1305→            # Get the full signal value
  1306→            full_value = signal_values[expr.name]
  1307→            width = signal_widths[expr.name]
  1308→
  1309→            if expr.msb is None:
  1310→                # Scalar reference
  1311→                return full_value, 1
  1312→            else:
  1313→                # Slice or single bit
  1314→                if expr.msb >= width or expr.lsb >= width:
  1315→                    raise IndexOutOfBoundsError(
  1316→                        f"Index {expr.msb}:{expr.lsb} out of bounds for signal {expr.name} (width {width})",
  1317→                        file=filepath
  1318→                    )
  1319→                # Extract bits from lsb to msb
  1320→                mask = ((1 << (expr.msb - expr.lsb + 1)) - 1) << expr.lsb
  1321→                slice_value = (full_value & mask) >> expr.lsb
  1322→                return slice_value, expr.msb - expr.lsb + 1
  1323→        else:
  1324→            raise UndefinedNameError(
  1325→                f"Undefined signal: {expr.name}",
  1326→                file=filepath
  1327→            )
  1328→
  1329→    elif isinstance(expr, Literal):
  1330→        return expr.value, expr.width
  1331→
  1332→    elif isinstance(expr, Concat):
  1333→        # Evaluate all parts
  1334→        parts_values = []
  1335→        total_width = 0
  1336→        for part in expr.parts:
  1337→            val, width = eval_expression(part, signal_values, signal_widths, filepath)
  1338→            parts_values.append((val, width))
  1339→            total_width += width
  1340→
  1341→        # Concatenate: first part is MSB
  1342→        result = 0
  1343→        for val, width in parts_values:
  1344→            result = (result << width) | val
  1345→
  1346→        return result, total_width
  1347→
  1348→    elif isinstance(expr, OpCall):
  1349→        op = expr.op.upper()
  1350→        args_results = []
  1351→        for arg in expr.args:
  1352→            val, width = eval_expression(arg, signal_values, signal_widths, filepath)
  1353→            args_results.append((val, width))
  1354→
  1355→        # Bitwise operators (width-preserving)
  1356→        if op in ('NOT', 'BUF'):
  1357→            if len(args_results) != 1:
  1358→                raise WidthMismatchError(
  1359→                    f"Operator {op} requires 1 operand, got {len(args_results)}",
  1360→                    file=filepath
  1361→                )
  1362→            val, width = args_results[0]
  1363→            result = (~val) & ((1 << width) - 1) if op == 'NOT' else val
  1364→            return result, width
  1365→
  1366→        elif op in ('AND', 'OR', 'XOR', 'NAND', 'NOR', 'XNOR'):
  1367→            if len(args_results) < 2:
  1368→                raise WidthMismatchError(
  1369→                    f"Operator {op} requires at least 2 operands, got {len(args_results)}",
  1370→                    file=filepath
  1371→                )
  1372→
  1373→            # Check all operands have same width
  1374→            first_width = args_results[0][1]
  1375→            for val, width in args_results:
  1376→                if width != first_width:
  1377→                    raise WidthMismatchError(
  1378→                        f"Operator {op}: operand widths {width} and {first_width} don't match",
  1379→                        file=filepath
  1380→                    )
  1381→
  1382→            mask = (1 << first_width) - 1
  1383→            result = args_results[0][0]
  1384→
  1385→            if op == 'AND':
  1386→                for val, _ in args_results[1:]:
  1387→                    result &= val
  1388→            elif op == 'OR':
  1389→                for val, _ in args_results[1:]:
  1390→                    result |= val
  1391→            elif op == 'XOR':
  1392→                for val, _ in args_results[1:]:
  1393→                    result ^= val
  1394→            elif op == 'NAND':
  1395→                for val, _ in args_results[1:]:
  1396→                    result &= val
  1397→                result = mask & ~result
  1398→            elif op == 'NOR':
  1399→                for val, _ in args_results[1:]:
  1400→                    result |= val
  1401→                result = mask & ~result
  1402→            elif op == 'XNOR':
  1403→                for val, _ in args_results[1:]:
  1404→                    result ^= val
  1405→                result = mask & ~result
  1406→
  1407→            return result & mask, first_width
  1408→
  1409→        elif op in ('MUX', 'ITE'):
  1410→            if len(args_results) != 3:
  1411→                raise ArityError(
  1412→                    f"Operator {op} requires 3 operands, got {len(args_results)}",
  1413→                    file=filepath
  1414→                )
  1415→
  1416→            sel_val, sel_width = args_results[0]
  1417→            a_val, a_width = args_results[1]
  1418→            b_val, b_width = args_results[2]
  1419→
  1420→            if sel_width != 1:
  1421→                raise WidthMismatchError(
  1422→                    f"Operator {op}: selector width {sel_width} must be 1",
  1423→                    file=filepath
  1424→                )
  1425→            if a_width != b_width:
  1426→                raise WidthMismatchError(
  1427→                    f"Operator {op}: operand widths {a_width} and {b_width} don't match",
  1428→                    file=filepath
  1429→                )
  1430→
  1431→            result = a_val if sel_val else b_val
  1432→            return result, a_width
  1433→
  1434→        elif op in ('REDUCE_AND', 'REDUCE_OR', 'REDUCE_XOR'):
  1435→            if len(args_results) != 1:
  1436→                raise WidthMismatchError(
  1437→                    f"Operator {op} requires 1 operand, got {len(args_results)}",
  1438→                    file=filepath
  1439→                )
  1440→
  1441→            val, width = args_results[0]
  1442→            if width < 1:
  1443→                raise WidthMismatchError(
  1444→                    f"Operator {op}: operand width {width} must be >= 1",
  1445→                    file=filepath
  1446→                )
  1447→
  1448→            mask = (1 << width) - 1
  1449→            val &= mask
  1450→
  1451→            if op == 'REDUCE_AND':
  1452→                # Check if all bits are 1
  1453→                result = 1 if val == mask else 0
  1454→            elif op == 'REDUCE_OR':
  1455→                result = 1 if val != 0 else 0
  1456→            else:  # REDUCE_XOR
  1457→                result = bin(val & mask).count('1') % 2
  1458→
  1459→            return result, 1
  1460→
  1461→        elif op == 'EQ':
  1462→            if len(args_results) != 2:
  1463→                raise ArityError(
  1464→                    f"Operator EQ requires 2 operands, got {len(args_results)}",
  1465→                    file=filepath
  1466→                )
  1467→
  1468→            a_val, a_width = args_results[0]
  1469→            b_val, b_width = args_results[1]
  1470→
  1471→            if a_width != b_width:
  1472→                raise WidthMismatchError(
  1473→                    f"Operator EQ: operand widths {a_width} and {b_width} don't match",
  1474→                    file=filepath
  1475→                )
  1476→
  1477→            return int(a_val == b_val), 1
  1478→
  1479→        else:
  1480→            raise ArityError(
  1481→                f"Unknown operator: {op}",
  1482→                file=filepath
  1483→            )
  1484→
  1485→    else:
  1486→        raise ValueError(f"Unknown AST node type: {type(expr)}")
  1487→
  1488→
  1489→def parse_input_literal(value_str: str, expected_width: int,
  1490→                        filepath: str, line: int = None, col: int = None) -> int:
  1491→    """Parse an input literal value and return the integer value.
  1492→
  1493→    Validates that the width matches the expected width.
  1494→    """
  1495→    if value_str in ('0', '1'):
  1496→        if expected_width != 1:
  1497→            raise InputWidthMismatchError(
  1498→                f"Input width mismatch: got 1 but expected {expected_width}",
  1499→                file=filepath, line=line, col=col
  1500→            )
  1501→        return int(value_str)
  1502→
  1503→    elif value_str.startswith('0b'):
  1504→        bits = value_str[2:]
  1505→        if not bits:
  1506→            raise InputValueParseError(
  1507→                f"Binary literal must have at least one digit",
  1508→                file=filepath, line=line, col=col
  1509→            )
  1510→        value = int(bits, 2)
  1511→        width = len(bits)
  1512→
  1513→        if width != expected_width:
  1514→            raise InputWidthMismatchError(
  1515→                f"Input width mismatch: got {width} but expected {expected_width}",
  1516→                file=filepath, line=line, col=col
  1517→            )
  1518→        return value
  1519→
  1520→    elif value_str.startswith('0x'):
  1521→        hex_digits = value_str[2:]
  1522→        if not hex_digits:
  1523→            raise InputValueParseError(
  1524→                f"Hex literal must have at least one digit",
  1525→                file=filepath, line=line, col=col
  1526→            )
  1527→        value = int(hex_digits, 16)
  1528→        width = len(hex_digits) * 4
  1529→
  1530→        if width != expected_width:
  1531→            raise InputWidthMismatchError(
  1532→                f"Input width mismatch: got {width} but expected {expected_width}",
  1533→                file=filepath, line=line, col=col
  1534→            )
  1535→        return value
  1536→
  1537→    else:
  1538→        # Sized literal: 8'b1010, 8'hff, 8'd200
  1539→        match = re.match(r'(\d+)\'[bBhHdD]([\d01a-fA-F_]+)', value_str)
  1540→        if match:
  1541→            width = int(match.group(1))
  1542→            radix_str = match.group(2).lower()
  1543→            value_str_clean = match.group(3).replace('_', '')
  1544→
  1545→            if width != expected_width:
  1546→                raise InputWidthMismatchError(
  1547→                    f"Input width mismatch: got {width} but expected {expected_width}",
  1548→                    file=filepath, line=line, col=col
  1549→                )
  1550→
  1551→            try:
  1552→                if radix_str == 'b':
  1553→                    value = int(value_str_clean, 2)
  1554→                elif radix_str == 'h':
  1555→                    value = int(value_str_clean, 16)
  1556→                else:  # 'd'
  1557→                    value = int(value_str_clean, 10)
  1558→            except ValueError:
  1559→                raise InputValueParseError(
  1560→                    f"Invalid literal: {value_str}",
  1561→                    file=filepath, line=line, col=col
  1562→                )
  1563→
  1564→            if value >= (1 << width):
  1565→                raise CircParseError(
  1566→                    f"Value {value} too large for {width}-bit literal",
  1567→                    file=filepath, line=line, col=col
  1568→                )
  1569→
  1570→            return value
  1571→        else:
  1572→            raise InputValueParseError(
  1573→                f"Invalid input literal format: {value_str}",
  1574→                file=filepath, line=line, col=col
  1575→            )
  1576→
  1577→
  1578→def format_vector_value(value: int, width: int, radix: str) -> str:
  1579→    """Format a vector value according to the specified radix.
  1580→
  1581→    Args:
  1582→        value: Integer value
  1583→        width: Bit width
  1584→        radix: 'bin', 'hex', or 'dec'
  1585→
  1586→    Returns:
  1587→        Formatted string
  1588→    """
  1589→    value &= (1 << width) - 1
  1590→
  1591→    if radix == 'bin':
  1592→        return f"0b{value:0{width}b}"
  1593→    elif radix == 'hex':
  1594→        # Calculate minimum number of hex digits
  1595→        hex_digits = (width + 3) // 4
  1596→        return f"0x{value:0{hex_digits}x}"
  1597→    elif radix == 'dec':
  1598→        return str(value)
  1599→

  1604→
  1605→def validate_circ_file(filepath: str, declarations: List[SignalDecl],
  1606→                       assignments: List[Tuple[str, ASTNode]]) -> Dict[str, object]:
  1607→    """Validate declarations and assignments, compute widths."""
  1608→
  1609→    # Track all declared signals
  1610→    inputs: Dict[str, SignalDecl] = {}
  1611→    outputs: Dict[str, SignalDecl] = {}
  1612→    wires: Dict[str, SignalDecl] = {}
  1613→    all_signals: Dict[str, SignalDecl] = {}
  1614→
  1615→    # Process declarations
  1616→    for decl in declarations:
  1617→        if decl.name in all_signals:
  1618→            raise DuplicateNameError(
  1619→                f"Duplicate signal name: {decl.name}",
  1620→                file=filepath
  1621→            )
  1622→        all_signals[decl.name] = decl
  1623→        if decl.sig_type == 'input':
  1624→            inputs[decl.name] = decl
  1625→        elif decl.sig_type == 'output':
  1626→            outputs[decl.name] = decl
  1627→        elif decl.sig_type == 'wire':
  1628→            wires[decl.name] = decl
  1629→
  1630→    # Track assignments
  1631→    assigned: Dict[str, Tuple[str, int]] = {}  # name -> (op, line_num)
  1632→
  1633→    # Build dependency graph for cycle detection
  1634→    graph: Dict[str, List[str]] = {}
  1635→
  1636→    # Operator arities
  1637→    op_arity = {
  1638→        'NOT': 1,
  1639→        'BUF': 1,
  1640→        'AND': 2,
  1641→        'OR': 2,
  1642→        'XOR': 2,
  1643→        'NAND': 2,
  1644→        'NOR': 2,
  1645→        'XNOR': 2,
  1646→        'MUX': 3,
  1647→        'ITE': 3,
  1648→        'REDUCE_AND': 1,
  1649→        'REDUCE_OR': 1,
  1650→        'REDUCE_XOR': 1,
  1651→        'EQ': 2,
  1652→    }
  1653→
  1654→    for i, (lhs, expr) in enumerate(assignments):
  1655→        line_num = i + 1
  1656→
  1657→        # Check LHS is valid
  1658→        if lhs not in all_signals:
  1659→            raise UndefinedNameError(
  1660→                f"Undefined signal: {lhs}",
  1661→                file=filepath
  1662→            )
  1663→
  1664→        signal_decl = all_signals[lhs]
  1665→        if signal_decl.sig_type == 'input':
  1666→            raise InputAssignmentError(
  1667→                f"Cannot assign to input: {lhs}",
  1668→                file=filepath
  1669→            )
  1670→
  1671→        # Check for multiple assignment
  1672→        if lhs in assigned:
  1673→            raise MultipleAssignmentError(
  1674→                f"Multiple assignment to: {lhs}",
  1675→                file=filepath
  1676→            )
  1677→
  1678→        assigned[lhs] = ('expr', line_num)
  1679→
  1680→        # Check expression and collect dependencies
  1681→        graph[lhs] = []
  1682→
  1683→        def collect_deps(node: ASTNode):
  1684→            if isinstance(node, SignalRef):
  1685→                if node.msb is not None:  # Has slice
  1686→                    # Check bounds against declared width if available
  1687→                    if node.name in all_signals:
  1688→                        decl = all_signals[node.name]
  1689→                        width = decl.width
  1690→                        if node.msb >= width or node.lsb >= width:
  1691→                            raise IndexOutOfBoundsError(
  1692→                                f"Index {node.msb}:{node.lsb} out of bounds for signal {node.name} (width {width})",
  1693→                                file=filepath
  1694→                            )
  1695→                if node.name not in ('0', '1'):
  1696→                    graph[lhs].append(node.name)
  1697→            elif isinstance(node, Literal):
  1698→                pass  # literals have no deps
  1699→            elif isinstance(node, Concat):
  1700→                for part in node.parts:
  1701→                    collect_deps(part)
  1702→            elif isinstance(node, OpCall):
  1703→                # Check arity
  1704→                op_upper = node.op.upper()
  1705→                if op_upper in op_arity:
  1706→                    min_arity = op_arity[op_upper]
  1707→                    if len(node.args) < min_arity:
  1708→                        raise ArityError(
  1709→                            f"Operator {node.op} requires at least {min_arity} operands, got {len(node.args)}",
  1710→                            file=filepath
  1711→                        )
  1712→                else:
  1713→                    raise ArityError(
  1714→                        f"Unknown operator: {node.op}",
  1715→                        file=filepath
  1716→                    )
  1717→                for arg in node.args:
  1718→                    collect_deps(arg)
  1719→
  1720→        try:
  1721→            collect_deps(expr)
  1722→        except (IndexOutOfBoundsError, ArityError):
  1723→            raise
  1724→        except Exception as e:
  1725→            raise CircParseError(
  1726→                f"Error in expression: {e}",
  1727→                file=filepath
  1728→            )
  1729→
  1730→    # Check for unassigned signals (only wires and outputs)
  1731→    for name, sig_type in [('wire', wires), ('output', outputs)]:
  1732→        for decl in all_signals.values():
  1733→            if decl.sig_type in ('wire', 'output'):
  1734→                if decl.name not in assigned:
  1735→                    raise UnassignedSignalError(
  1736→                        f"Unassigned signal: {decl.name}",
  1737→                        file=filepath
  1738→                    )
  1739→
  1740→    # Check for cycles
  1741→    def find_cycle() -> Optional[List[str]]:
  1742→        visited: Set[str] = set()
  1743→        rec_stack: List[str] = []
  1744→
  1745→        def dfs(node: str) -> Optional[List[str]]:
  1746→            visited.add(node)
  1747→            rec_stack.append(node)
  1748→
  1749→            for neighbor in graph.get(node, []):
  1750→                if neighbor not in visited:
  1751→                    result = dfs(neighbor)
  1752→                    if result:
  1753→                        return result
  1754→                elif neighbor in rec_stack:
  1755→                    start_idx = rec_stack.index(neighbor)
  1756→                    return rec_stack[start_idx:] + [node]
  1757→
  1758→            rec_stack.pop()
  1759→            return None
  1760→
  1761→        for node in graph:
  1762→            if node not in visited:
  1763→                cycle = dfs(node)
  1764→                if cycle:
  1765→                    return cycle
  1766→        return None
  1767→
  1768→    cycle = find_cycle()
  1769→    if cycle:
  1770→        cycle_path = " -> ".join(cycle)
  1771→        raise CycleError(
  1772→            f"Cycle detected: {cycle_path}",
  1773→            file=filepath
  1774→        )
  1775→
  1776→    # Return success info
  1777→    sorted_inputs = sorted(inputs.values(), key=lambda d: d.name)
  1778→    sorted_outputs = sorted(outputs.values(), key=lambda d: d.name)
  1779→
  1780→    return {
  1781→        'inputs': [{'name': d.name, 'msb': d.msb, 'lsb': d.lsb} for d in sorted_inputs],
  1782→        'outputs': [{'name': d.name, 'msb': d.msb, 'lsb': d.lsb} for d in sorted_outputs],
  1783→    }
  1784→

  1813→def detect_format(filepath: str, format_override: str) -> str:
  1814→    """Detect circuit format from file extension or override.
  1815→
  1816→    Args:
  1817→        filepath: Path to the circuit file
  1818→        format_override: One of 'auto', 'circ', 'json', 'bench'
  1819→
  1820→    Returns:
  1821→        Format string: 'circ', 'json', or 'bench'
  1822→
  1823→    Raises:
  1824→        UnknownInputFormatError: If format cannot be determined
  1825→    """
  1826→    if format_override != 'auto':
  1827→        return format_override
  1828→
  1829→    # Determine from file extension
  1830→    _, ext = os.path.splitext(filepath)
  1831→    ext = ext.lower()
  1832→
  1833→    if ext == '.circ':
  1834→        return 'circ'
  1835→    elif ext == '.json':
  1836→        return 'json'
  1837→    elif ext == '.bench':
  1838→        return 'bench'
  1839→    else:
  1840→        raise UnknownInputFormatError(
  1841→            f"Unknown input format for file: {filepath}",
  1842→            file=filepath
  1843→        )
  1844→

  1846→def cmd_check(filepath: str, json_output: bool, format_flag: str) -> int:
  1847→    """Execute check command."""
  1848→    # Check file exists first
  1849→    if not os.path.exists(filepath):
  1850→        if json_output:
  1851→            print(format_error_json(
  1852→                FileNotFoundError(f"File not found: {filepath}", filepath),
  1853→                'check'
  1854→            ))
  1855→        else:
  1856→            print(f"Error: File not found: {filepath}", file=sys.stderr)
  1857→        return 1
  1858→
  1859→    try:
  1860→        fmt = detect_format(filepath, format_flag)
  1861→
  1862→        if fmt == 'circ':
  1863→            declarations, assignments = parse_circ_file(filepath)
  1864→        elif fmt == 'json':
  1865→            declarations, assignments = parse_json_file(filepath)
  1866→        elif fmt == 'bench':
  1867→            declarations, assignments = parse_bench_file(filepath)
  1868→        else:
  1869→            raise UnknownInputFormatError(
  1870→                f"Unsupported format: {fmt}",
  1871→                file=filepath
  1872→            )
  1873→
  1874→        result = validate_circ_file(filepath, declarations, assignments)
  1875→
  1876→        if json_output:
  1877→            output = {
  1878→                'ok': True,
  1879→                'command': 'check',
  1880→                'format': fmt,
  1881→                'inputs': result['inputs'],
  1882→                'outputs': result['outputs'],
  1883→            }
  1884→            print(json.dumps(output))
  1885→        else:
  1886→            input_strs = []
  1887→            for i in result['inputs']:
  1888→                if i['msb'] is not None:
  1889→                    input_strs.append(f"{i['name']}[{i['msb']}:{i['lsb']}]")
  1890→                else:
  1891→                    input_strs.append(i['name'])
  1892→            output_strs = []
  1893→            for o in result['outputs']:
  1894→                if o['msb'] is not None:
  1895→                    output_strs.append(f"{o['name']}[{o['msb']}:{o['lsb']}]")
  1896→                else:
  1897→                    output_strs.append(o['name'])
  1898→            print(f"Inputs: {', '.join(input_strs)}")
  1899→            print(f"Outputs: {', '.join(output_strs)}")
  1900→
  1901→        return 0
  1902→
  1903→    except CircuitError as e:
  1904→        if json_output:
  1905→            print(format_error_json(e, 'check'))
  1906→        else:
  1907→            print(f"Error: {e.message}", file=sys.stderr)
  1908→        return e.exit_code

  1915→def get_dependencies(expr: ASTNode) -> List[str]:
  1916→    """Extract signal dependencies from an expression recursively.
  1917→
  1918→    For indexed/sliced references, the dependency is on the base signal.
  1919→    Returns a list of signal names (with duplicates).
  1920→    """
  1921→    deps = []
  1922→
  1923→    if isinstance(expr, SignalRef):
  1924→        deps.append(expr.name)
  1925→    elif isinstance(expr, Concat):
  1926→        for part in expr.parts:
  1927→            deps.extend(get_dependencies(part))
  1928→    elif isinstance(expr, OpCall):
  1929→        for arg in expr.args:
  1930→            deps.extend(get_dependencies(arg))
  1931→
  1932→    return deps
  1933→
  1934→
  1935→def classify_expression(expr: ASTNode) -> str:
  1936→    """Classify an expression for op_histogram categories."""
  1937→    if isinstance(expr, SignalRef):
  1938→        return "BUF"
  1939→    elif isinstance(expr, Literal):
  1940→        return "BUF"
  1941→    elif isinstance(expr, Concat):
  1942→        return "CONCAT"
  1943→    elif isinstance(expr, OpCall):
  1944→        return expr.op.upper()
  1945→    else:
  1946→        return "BUF"
  1947→
  1948→
  1949→def compute_depth(declarations: List[SignalDecl],
  1950→                  assignments: List[Tuple[str, ASTNode]],
  1951→                  inputs: Dict[str, SignalDecl],
  1952→                  outputs: Dict[str, SignalDecl]) -> int:
  1953→    """Compute the maximum depth from any input to any output."""
  1954→    # Build a map of assignment LHS to its depth
  1955→    # For each assignment: depth = 1 + max(depth of dependencies)
  1956→    # If no dependencies (BUF from input/literal), depth = 1
  1957→
  1958→    # Build dependency graph
  1959→    graph: Dict[str, List[str]] = {}
  1960→    dep_counts: Dict[str, int] = {}  # Track number of dependencies
  1961→
  1962→    def collect_deps(node: ASTNode):
  1963→        if isinstance(node, SignalRef):
  1964→            return [node.name]
  1965→        elif isinstance(node, Concat):
  1966→            deps = []
  1967→            for part in node.parts:
  1968→                deps.extend(collect_deps(part))
  1969→            return deps
  1970→        elif isinstance(node, OpCall):
  1971→            deps = []
  1972→            for arg in node.args:
  1973→                deps.extend(collect_deps(arg))
  1974→            return deps
  1975→        return []
  1976→
  1977→    # Map LHS signal names to their dependencies
  1978→    signal_to_depth: Dict[str, int] = {}
  1979→
  1980→    # Process in order
  1981→    for lhs, expr in assignments:
  1982→        deps = collect_deps(expr)
  1983→        signal_to_depth[lhs] = deps
  1984→
  1985→    # Compute topological order
  1986→    # Simple iterative approach
  1987→    depth_map: Dict[str, int] = {}
  1988→
  1989→    def get_depth(signal: str) -> int:
  1990→        if signal in depth_map:
  1991→            return depth_map[signal]
  1992→
  1993→        # If it's an input, depth is 0 (we count from input as depth 0)
  1994→        if signal in inputs:
  1995→            return 0
  1996→
  1997→        # If it's an assignment LHS
  1998→        if signal in signal_to_depth:
  1999→            deps = signal_to_depth[signal]
  2000→            if not deps:
  2001→                depth_map[signal] = 1
  2002→                return 1
  2003→
  2004→            max_dep_depth = 0
  2005→            for dep in deps:
  2006→                max_dep_depth = max(max_dep_depth, get_depth(dep))
  2007→
  2008→            depth_map[signal] = max_dep_depth + 1
  2009→            return depth_map[signal]
  2010→
  2011→        # Should not happen for valid circuits
  2012→        return 0
  2013→
  2014→    # Compute depth for all signals that are outputs (directly or indirectly)
  2015→    max_depth = 0
  2016→    for out_name in outputs:
  2017→        max_depth = max(max_depth, get_depth(out_name))
  2018→
  2019→    return max_depth
  2020→

  2022→def cmd_stats(filepath: str, json_output: bool, format_flag: str) -> int:
  2023→    """Execute stats command."""
  2024→    # Check file exists first
  2025→    if not os.path.exists(filepath):
  2026→        if json_output:
  2027→            print(format_error_json(
  2028→                FileNotFoundError(f"File not found: {filepath}", filepath),
  2029→                'stats'
  2030→            ))
  2031→        else:
  2032→            print(f"Error: File not found: {filepath}", file=sys.stderr)
  2033→        return 1
  2034→
  2035→    try:
  2036→        fmt = detect_format(filepath, format_flag)
  2037→
  2038→        if fmt == 'circ':
  2039→            declarations, assignments = parse_circ_file(filepath)
  2040→        elif fmt == 'json':
  2041→            declarations, assignments = parse_json_file(filepath)
  2042→        elif fmt == 'bench':
  2043→            declarations, assignments = parse_bench_file(filepath)
  2044→        else:
  2045→            raise UnknownInputFormatError(
  2046→                f"Unsupported format: {fmt}",
  2047→                file=filepath
  2048→            )
  2049→
  2050→        # Get validated information
  2051→        result = validate_circ_file(filepath, declarations, assignments)
  2052→        inputs = {d.name: d for d in declarations if d.sig_type == 'input'}
  2053→        outputs = {d.name: d for d in declarations if d.sig_type == 'output'}
  2054→        wires = {d.name: d for d in declarations if d.sig_type == 'wire'}
  2055→
  2056→        # Count metrics
  2057→        inputs_count = len(inputs)
  2058→        outputs_count = len(outputs)
  2059→        wires_count = len(wires)
  2060→        assignments_count = len(assignments)
  2061→
  2062→        # Count edges (with duplicates)
  2063→        edges_count = 0
  2064→        for _, expr in assignments:
  2065→            deps = get_dependencies(expr)
  2066→            edges_count += len(deps)
  2067→
  2068→        # Compute depth
  2069→        depth = compute_depth(declarations, assignments, inputs, outputs)
  2070→
  2071→        # Compute op histogram
  2072→        op_categories = ['BUF', 'CONCAT', 'AND', 'OR', 'XOR', 'NAND', 'NOR', 'XNOR',
  2073→                         'NOT', 'MUX', 'ITE', 'REDUCE_AND', 'REDUCE_OR', 'REDUCE_XOR', 'EQ']
  2074→        op_histogram = {cat: 0 for cat in op_categories}
  2075→
  2076→        for _, expr in assignments:
  2077→            category = classify_expression(expr)
  2078→            op_histogram[category] += 1
  2079→
  2080→        if json_output:
  2081→            output = {
  2082→                'ok': True,
  2083→                'command': 'stats',
  2084→                'inputs_count': inputs_count,
  2085→                'outputs_count': outputs_count,
  2086→                'wires_count': wires_count,
  2087→                'assignments_count': assignments_count,
  2088→                'edges_count': edges_count,
  2089→                'depth': depth,
  2090→                'op_histogram': op_histogram,
  2091→            }
  2092→            print(json.dumps(output))
  2093→        else:
  2094→            print(f"inputs_count: {inputs_count}")
  2095→            print(f"outputs_count: {outputs_count}")
  2096→            print(f"wires_count: {wires_count}")
  2097→            print(f"assignments_count: {assignments_count}")
  2098→            print(f"edges_count: {edges_count}")
  2099→            print(f"depth: {depth}")
  2100→            print("op_histogram:")
  2101→            for cat, count in sorted(op_histogram.items()):
  2102→                print(f"  {cat}: {count}")
  2103→
  2104→        return 0
  2105→
  2106→    except CircuitError as e:
  2107→        if json_output:
  2108→            print(format_error_json(e, 'stats'))
  2109→        else:
  2110→            print(f"Error: {e.message}", file=sys.stderr)
  2111→        return e.exit_code

  2137→def lint_unused_inputs(declarations: List[SignalDecl],
